spec_match.rationale: Exercises column-rule/row-rule across grid and flex and now exposes the `repeat()` rule-style syntax that the feature index calls out. The `gap-rule` shorthand is still not surfaced.

interactive.rationale: Real controls (layout tabs, range sliders, selects, colour pickers, and a repeat-mode toggle) drive a live preview and emit copyable CSS — well past the static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises an interactive builder with live preview and copyable snippet; the page delivers exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is covered; zero-width handled by collapsing to `none`, but no demo of `style=none`, `groove`/`ridge` quirks, sub-pixel widths, or layouts where the new properties have no effect (single column / single row).

references.rationale: The `see also` section links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5157805733183488 directly.

design_system.rationale: All colours come from CSS variables, the copy button overrides background + color together, and there is no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Surface the `gap-rule` shorthand
openQuestions.detail: Once both column-rule and row-rule are set, output a single `gap-rule: ...` shorthand line alongside the longhand. That is the canonical authoring sugar in the css-gaps-1 spec and is currently invisible in the builder.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate `style: none` and width-zero collapse
openQuestions.detail: Edge cases worth showing: setting style to `none` should hide the rule even if width > 0; width 0 should hide it even if style is solid. Right now both code paths emit the same `none` value, which obscures the spec behaviour. Add a tooltip or a small `actual rendered rule` readout.

openQuestions.title: Add a no-overlap probe for shared-edge collapsing
openQuestions.detail: A long-standing pain with borders is double-stacking at cell edges. The builder should include a `compare with border` toggle so authors can see the column-rule (single line) vs border (double thickness at every internal edge) side by side — that contrast is the whole point of the feature.

summary: The builder now demonstrates the headline `repeat()` syntax for alternating column separators while retaining live preview and copyable CSS. Minor follow-ups remain for `gap-rule` shorthand output and deeper edge-case visualization.
